title: Universal Habit Tracker App — Full Initial Build

requirements.text: Create a Motoko backend actor that stores and manages all habit-related data: habit definitions (id, name, type, unit, icon, color, target, frequency), daily log entries (habitId, date, value, notes), user streaks (current streak, longest streak per habit), and user theme preference. Expose CRUD endpoints for habits, log entries, and theme preference. Include query functions for fetching entries by date range for analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: Can create, read, update, and delete habit definitions
Can log a value entry for a habit on a given date
Can retrieve all log entries for a habit within a date range
Streak data is calculated and returned per habit
User theme preference is persisted and retrievable

requirements.text: Implement eight built-in habit log types with appropriate default units, icons, and input controls: Water (ml/oz), Calories (kcal), Exercise (minutes/reps), Weight (kg/lbs), Sleep (hours), Study (minutes), Pills (count/checkbox), and To-Dos (task list with completion toggle). Each type should have a type-specific input UI.
acceptanceCriteria: All eight built-in types are available when creating a new habit
Each type shows a contextually appropriate input control (slider, number input, checkbox, task list, etc.)
Default units and icons are pre-filled for each built-in type
Pills type supports checkbox-style completion
To-Dos type supports adding, completing, and removing individual tasks

requirements.text: Build a custom log builder that allows users to define their own habit type with a name, unit label, icon (from a preset icon set), color, daily target value, and input type (numeric, boolean, or text). Custom habits are stored alongside built-in habits.
acceptanceCriteria: User can open a 'Create Custom Habit' modal or page
User can set name, unit label, icon, color, target, and input type
Custom habits appear in the main habit list after creation
Custom habits can be edited and deleted

requirements.text: Implement streak tracking for each habit: calculate current consecutive-day streak and longest-ever streak based on log entries. Display a GitHub-style contribution heatmap calendar (365 days) for each habit showing log frequency and intensity.
acceptanceCriteria: Current streak and longest streak are shown on each habit card
Streaks are recalculated when new entries are logged
Heatmap renders 365 days with color intensity based on entry value relative to target
Days with no entry appear as empty/muted cells
Heatmap is accessible from each habit's detail view

requirements.text: Add animated progress rings (circular progress indicators) to each habit card on the dashboard showing today's completion percentage relative to the daily target. Rings should animate on load and on value update.
acceptanceCriteria: Each habit card displays a circular progress ring
Ring fill percentage reflects today's logged value vs. target
Ring animates smoothly when value changes
Ring shows 100% filled state with a distinct color when target is met
Percentage label is shown inside the ring

requirements.text: Build an analytics page with charts for each habit showing: a line/bar chart of daily values over the past 30 days, a weekly average bar chart, and a goal completion rate percentage. Use a charting library such as Recharts.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics page is accessible from the main navigation
User can select a habit to view its analytics
30-day daily value chart renders correctly with dates on x-axis
Weekly average chart groups data by week
Goal completion rate is displayed as a percentage stat card
Charts are responsive and readable on mobile

requirements.text: Implement a full theme system with five distinct pre-built visual themes — Minimal (clean whites, soft grays), Glassmorphism (frosted glass cards, blur effects), Neon Dark (dark background, vivid neon accents), Nature (earthy greens and warm browns), and Retro (pixel-inspired, warm yellows and reds) — switchable from a settings panel. Each theme defines color palette, border radii, card styles, and typography weight.
acceptanceCriteria: A settings panel or theme picker allows switching between all five themes
Theme change is applied globally and immediately without page reload
Each theme has a visually distinct appearance across all components
Glassmorphism theme uses backdrop-blur and semi-transparent cards
Neon Dark theme uses dark backgrounds with glowing accent colors
Selected theme is persisted via backend and restored on next visit

requirements.text: Add a custom theme builder that lets users define their own theme by choosing primary color, background color, accent color, card style (flat/glass/shadow), and border radius level (none/small/large/pill). Preview the theme live before saving.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom theme builder is accessible from the theme settings panel
User can pick colors using a color picker for primary, background, and accent
Card style and border radius options are selectable
Live preview updates the UI as options are changed
Saved custom theme appears as a selectable option alongside built-in themes

requirements.text: Integrate rich animations throughout the app using Framer Motion: page transitions, habit card enter/exit animations, progress ring draws, log entry success micro-animations (confetti or pulse effect on hitting a goal), and staggered list animations for the habit dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Page/view transitions use smooth Framer Motion animations
Habit cards animate in with staggered entry on dashboard load
Progress ring fill animates on mount and on value update
A celebratory micro-animation plays when a daily goal is completed
Log entry submission has a confirmation animation
All animations respect reduced-motion preferences

requirements.text: Design and enforce a cohesive visual theme that adapts to each selected style while maintaining a premium, modern feel. The default theme (Minimal) should use a crisp off-white and charcoal palette with clean card layouts, generous spacing, and a bold sans-serif typeface. Navigation should be a fixed bottom tab bar on mobile and a left sidebar on desktop.
acceptanceCriteria: Default Minimal theme is applied on first load
Bottom tab bar navigation is shown on narrow screens
Left sidebar navigation is shown on wide screens
Typography is bold and legible across all themes
Spacing and layout feel premium and uncluttered

constraints: All backend logic must reside in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo)
Do not use any external databases or cloud services
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui/
Do not use blue & purple as the primary palette in any theme
nonGoals: User authentication via Internet Identity (no login required for this build)
Integration with external health APIs (Apple Health, Google Fit, etc.)

required: App logo mark — a circular gradient ring icon with a checkmark inside, modern flat style (logo-mark.dim_256x256.png)
Onboarding hero illustration — abstract flowing lines and glowing rings on a dark background suggesting progress and habit building (onboarding-hero.dim_800x400.png)
